meta.title: Unit 3 — Pronouns & I/me (Test 3)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Focus on I vs. me and replacing nouns with the correct pronouns.

questions.question: Which pronoun fills the blank? ___ am eight years old.
questions.explanation: "I am" — "I" is the subject pronoun used before the verb.

questions.question: "I" is used as a subject (before the verb).
questions.explanation: Yes! "I" is the subject pronoun — it always goes before the verb.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: My mom calls I every day.
My mom calls me every day.
My mom calls my every day.
My mom calls mine every day.
questions.explanation: "me" comes after the verb (object pronoun). My mom calls me.

questions.question: "She" is used to replace a male person's name.
questions.explanation: "She" is used for females. "He" is used for males.

questions.question: The cactus is green and prickly. → ___ is green and prickly.
questions.explanation: "the cactus" is a plant/thing — we use "It" to replace it.

questions.question: Fill in: ___ see an old basket. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "I" is the subject — it goes before the verb. "I see an old basket."

questions.question: Mina's brother finds a cactus. → ___ finds a cactus.
questions.explanation: Mina's brother is male — we use "He".

questions.question: Sort these sentences: does the blank need 'I' or 'me'?
questions.explanation: 'I' = subject (before verb). 'me' = object (after verb).

questions.question: In the sentence "I have a class every Monday," the pronoun "I" is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! "I" is the subject before the verb "have". This is correct.

questions.question: Fill in: Please give ___ the book. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "me" is the object — it comes after the verb. "Please give me the book."

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "I have a class every Monday." 'I' is the subject pronoun.

questions.question: Andy is my friend. ___ is kind.
questions.explanation: Andy is male — we use "He" to replace Andy's name.

questions.question: The sentence "Me am not scared of the lion" is correct.
questions.explanation: "Me am" is wrong. "I" is the subject: "I am not scared of the lion."

questions.question: Mom is good at cooking. ___ is the best chef at home.
questions.explanation: Mom is female — we use "She" to replace Mom.

questions.question: Fill in: Rosa and ___ paint it. (I or me)
questions.hint: 'Rosa and ___' is the subject of the sentence
questions.explanation: "I" is used as part of the subject 'Rosa and I' — it comes before the verb.

questions.question: Sort these: which pronoun replaces the underlined noun?
questions.explanation: He = male person. It = thing/animal. They = multiple people or things.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'I' and 'me' correctly?
options: Can you give I the pencil?
Can you give me the pencil?
Can me give I the pencil?
Can I give me the pencil?
questions.explanation: "me" is the object (after the verb 'give'). "Can you give me the pencil?"

questions.question: The sentence "I love dogs and cats" uses the correct pronoun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "I" is the subject before the verb "love". This is correct.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "She always helps me with homework." 'me' is the object after the verb.

questions.question: Which sentence has a mistake?
options: My mom calls me every day.
Can you give I the pencil?
questions.explanation: "give I" is wrong — after a verb, use 'me': "Can you give me the pencil?"
